title: EUV dose reduction achievement gives away crucial limitation on dose/focus control
gist: Citing 2026 papers from Tokyo Electron (TEL) and Samsung/Synopsys, the post reports that unabsorbed EUV light passing through resist into the underlayer releases electrons that significantly contribute to resist exposure — TEL cut dose-to-size for 28nm pitch lines nearly 50% (81.4 to 42.6 mJ/cm2) by changing the underlayer — meaning underlayer electron yield, not just resist chemistry, is now a critical, previously underappreciated lever on EUV lithography performance.

themes.theme: Underlayer electron exposure as a hidden EUV lithography variable

insights.claim: TEL found that dose-to-size for 28 nm pitch lines and spaces was reduced from 81.4 mJ/cm2 to 42.6 mJ/cm2 — nearly 50% — simply by changing the underlayer beneath the EUV resist, demonstrating that electrons generated in the underlayer (not just the resist itself) substantially contribute to resist exposure.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A ~50% dose-to-size reduction via underlayer engineering alone is a major potential lever for EUV throughput/cost improvement (lower dose = faster exposure = higher wafer throughput), relevant to lithography process and materials suppliers.
insights.quote: TEL found that dose to size for 28 nm pitch lines and spaces was reduced from 81.4 mJ/cm2 to 42.6 mJ/cm2, nearly 50%, by changing the underlayer.

insights.claim: Because underlayer electron exposure originates from some distance below the resist layer, it comes from a defocused, blurred image even when the primary optical image is correctly focused within the resist — meaning the underlayer's electron yield and mean free path have become the most significant factor in EUV lithography performance.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Identifies a previously underappreciated physical mechanism (blurred, defocused underlayer electron contribution) that constrains achievable pattern fidelity independent of optical focus quality — a technical constraint materials and resist vendors must now engineer around as pitches continue to shrink.
insights.quote: Therefore, the underlayer's electron yield and mean free path has become the most significant factor in EUV lithography performance.
